promoter_salary.title: Promoter Salary
promoter_salary.description:
### **Promoter Salary & Remuneration**

* **Where to look:** Look into data on employee expenses - which depict the amount of salary taken by employees and management over historical time periods.
* **What info to look at:** Ensure the management's salary is in line with company profits. The management's salary percentage should not drastically increase. 
* **Red Flag:** If profits or sales are decreasing but employee costs (salaries) are significantly increasing, it indicates poor management priorities .
*

board_of_directors.title: Board of Directors
board_of_directors.description:
### **Composition of Board of Directors**
* **What info to look at:** Check the ratio of **Executive** (insiders) vs. **Non-Executive/Independent** Directors in the governance section of the annual report.
* **Ideal Setup:** At least 50% of the board should be Independent Directors (industry experts with no conflict of interest). This prevents a single person (like a CEO) from having absolute, unchecked power.
* **Red Flag:** Boards filled with family members (uncles, cousins) who only exist to sign papers.

management_background.title: Management Background
management_background.description:
### **Background & Character Check**

* **What to search:** Use Web Search to search for the share alongside keywords like **"fraud," "scandal," "dispute,"** or **"court case"**.
* **What to look for:** Focus on the character and integrity of the management rather than just their educational qualifications. Look for red flags in their past behavior or any history of financial irregularities or problems with regulatory bodies like sebi.

promoter_shareholding.title: Promoter Shareholding
promoter_shareholding.description:
### **Promoter Skin in the Game**

* **Where to look:** Fetch data on shareholding pattern - which depict the shareholding or ownership percentage by promoters/management over historical time periods.
* **What info to look at:** Look for the trend in promoter holdings.
* **Positive:** Promoters increasing their stake shows confidence.
* **Negative:** Promoters consistently reducing their stake may mean they don't believe in the company’s future.

transcripts_qna.title: Conference Call Transcripts QnA
transcripts_qna.description:
### **Earnings Call/ Conference Calls (Con-Calls)**

* **Where to look:** Fetch and read Read the **"Con-call Transcripts"**.
* **What info to look at:** Read the Q&A section where fund managers question the management. This provides clarity on specific operational doubts .
* Are the management able to handle tough questions ?
* Are there any red flags about the management or company ?
* Are the management and owners able to reply to the questions with confidence and clarity ?
* Do the company owner sound optimistic or pessimistic ?
* Is the company able to meet previous targets?

vision.title: Management Vision
vision.description:
### **Future Vision & Strategy**
* **Where to look:** In the Annual Report, read the **"Director’s Report," "Letter to Shareholders,"** and the **"Management Discussion and Analysis (MD&A)"**.
* **What info to look at:** Look for clarity on where the company is heading. For example, is a paint company diversifying into home decor? Assess if these growth plans are logical and achievable.

sentiment.title: Market/ Investor Sentiment
sentiment.description: Are other investors bullish etc, DII FII holdings ?
